name: MongoDB Atlas
description: Managed, cloud-hosted MongoDB database service with administration APIs for programmatic management of clusters, projects, and account-level resources.

auth_methods.note: Atlas supports Public/Private Key (HTTP Digest) API keys, OAuth2 service-account tokens, and the docs explicitly describe these. The schema does not include 'digest_auth' as a value, so 'HTTP Digest (public/private key)' is represented here as 'other'. 'none_public' kept only for clarity although Atlas administration API generally requires authentication.

credential_access.note: The 'Create an API Key' tutorial shows how a user in the Atlas UI can create API keys without human approval. Keys are created by project owners/organization admins; signup and free tier are available.

api_surface.note: Atlas exposes a versioned REST administration API (base URL https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/) with many endpoints covering clusters, projects, users, alerts, backup, etc.

buildability.note: Public REST API docs plus self-serve API key and OAuth2 service-account flows exist; credentials can be obtained via the Atlas UI or service account token flow.

queries: MongoDB Atlas API documentation root site:mongodb.com/docs/atlas/api
